event: transferred to the neonatal intensive care unit
minimal enteral nutrition with breast milk
Laboratory and radiological findings were compatible with NEC
operated on the postnatal sixth day
circulatory disturbances in the intestinal wall
long segment including the jejunum and ileum was resected
stoma was formed with the proximal end
distal end was left closed in the abdomen
TPN until the postoperative seventh day
TPN support could not be discontinued
serum total bilirubin level was 12.12 mg/dL
predominant component being direct reacting bilirubin (DB: 11.48 mg/dL)
no response to treatment during follow-up
enteral dose of levothyroxine was increased to 10 µg/kg/day
still no increase in fT4 levels
parenteral and suppository levothyroxine preparations were not available
the tablet form of the drug was ground
diluted with 10 mL of saline to be administered rectally
at a dose of 10 µg/kg/day (4 mL/kg) by a 6 Fr feeding tube
After nine days of rectal levothyroxine treatment fT4 levels increased
